title: Crypto-D1 Kraken Normalize v1 -- offline operator tool (implementation report)
purpose: Normalize operator-supplied, manually-downloaded Kraken daily OHLCVT exports (BTC/ETH/SOL spot, USD) into the single canonical Crypto-D1 dataset, with offline-only, write-jailed, freeze-capable behavior. This tool does NOT fetch, run QA, run a backtest, or trade.
approved_normalization_decisions: Offline only; reads only files passed via --raw-dir; no network/subprocess/credentials.
Dry-run is the default; --write materializes; --freeze (requires --write) emits CHECKSUMS.txt + FREEZE_RECORD.txt.
Write-jail: every write path is resolved and confined to the single dataset directory.
Symbol aliases: XBTUSD/XXBTZUSD->BTC, ETHUSD/XETHZUSD->ETH, SOLUSD->SOL; all USD-quoted.
Timestamps converted from Kraken unix-seconds bar-open to ISO-8601 UTC (YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z).
Range filtered to 2021-06-17..2025-12-31 inclusive; out-of-range/partial-day bars excluded.
One combined daily_ohlcv.csv for all three assets; sorted by asset (BTC,ETH,SOL) then timestamp.
Prices kept as source strings to avoid precision loss; validated numerically for OHLC self-consistency.
fees.json ships a PLACEHOLDER_FILL_BEFORE_QA sentinel; manifest QA_status stays QA_DRAFT until filled.
Manifest freeze_status=FROZEN only under --freeze; operator label 'Mahmoud Cherif — operator manual Kraken intake'; FREEZE_RECORD pins versions + Kraken TOS evidence reference.
integrity_rules_enforced: duplicate (symbol, timestamp) rejected as a hard error
all three required asset files must be present
single raw source file per asset

tests.fixtures: synthetic only; every write test points --repo-root at a pytest tmp_path

distinctions: FROZEN means checksummed and immutable; it does NOT mean QA-passed.
Clean daily OHLCV data does not imply profit.
A good historical chart does not imply future returns.
This tool does not authorize a backtest, paper trading, or live trading.
not_done_in_this_step: Did not copy raw Kraken data into SPARTA.
Did not start paper or live trading.
